page: Microsoft Azure F80ams_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the Famsv7 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor operating at 4.5 GHz, delivering 80 dedicated physical cores and 80 vCPUs with a 1.0 thread-per-core allocation. The instance is equipped with 640.0 GB of system memory, providing a high resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance across multiple domains. The single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng, along with PassMark CPU scores, rank in the top 10%. Memory subsystem performance is highly efficient, with cached small-block read bandwidth reaching 14,097.63 GB/sec and uncached large-block write bandwidth at 214.02 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server demonstrates strong capabilities in LLM inference, processing 349.11 tokens/sec for medium (7B) model prompts and 54.05 tokens/sec for text generation, though performance drops to an average tier for large (70B) model prompt processing.

Given the dedicated core allocation, high clock speed, and substantial memory capacity, this instance offers high resource density for memory-intensive and compute-heavy workloads. The absence of bundled local storage and GPUs means cost efficiency is optimized for workloads relying on network-attached Premium Storage and CPU-bound processing. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, memory-intensive analytics, high-throughput web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F80ams_v7 is a memory-intensive server instance from the Famsv7 family, featuring 80 dedicated vCPUs and physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. With 640.0 GB of RAM, it delivers a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, as well as memory bandwidth, with cached read speeds reaching 14,097.63 GB/sec. It also demonstrates strong performance in LLM inference for small and medium models. This hardware profile is optimized for memory-intensive databases, high-throughput web serving, and demanding enterprise workloads requiring dedicated compute resources without local storage overhead.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F80ams_v7 is a memory-intensive server featuring 80 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 4.5 GHz, 640 GB of RAM, and top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F80ams_v7 is an 80-core AMD EPYC server with 640 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive and high-compute workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency eighty-core processor paired with substantial memory capacity for demanding database and compute-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 80 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 physical cores operating at 4.5 GHz
640.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
Premium Storage capable with no bundled local storage or GPUs
Suited for memory-intensive databases, enterprise applications, and LLM inference
